---
description: 'Scaffold an AI Agent plugin (ai_agents determine/solve framework)'
argument-hint: '<module_path> <AgentName>'
agent: 'agent'
---

<!-- GENERATED by bin/build-adapters from plugins/drupal-dkan-ai — do not edit; re-run the script -->


Scaffold a new AI Agent plugin for the Drupal AI module — autonomous worker that uses LLM-driven YAML prompts to fulfill a goal. Uses the `ai_agents` framework's "determine → solve" pattern, not OpenAI-style tool-calling loops.

Read [SKILL.md](../../plugins/drupal-dkan-ai/skills/drupal-ai-module/SKILL.md), [plugin-types.md](../../plugins/drupal-dkan-ai/skills/drupal-ai-module/reference/plugin-types.md#ai-agent), and [services.md](../../plugins/drupal-dkan-ai/skills/drupal-ai-module/reference/services.md#agent-plugin) before proceeding. Then read `<webroot>/modules/contrib/ai_agents/src/Plugin/AiAgent/FieldType.php` as the canonical example — the scaffold below produces only a minimal skeleton.

## Input

`${input:args}` should be: `<module_path> <AgentName>`

- `module_path`: Path to the target module — relative to the project root (e.g. `<webroot>/modules/custom/my_module`) or just the machine name (e.g. `dkan_query_tools`), resolved against the site's module directories.
- `AgentName`: PascalCase class name (e.g., `DatasetExplorer`). The class name typically does NOT include "Agent" suffix — match the built-in convention (`FieldType`, `ContentType`, `TaxonomyAgent`).

## Steps

### 1. Version gate

Check `composer show drupal/ai_agents`. Refuse on `2.x` (breaking changes). Refuse if not installed: "drupal/ai_agents not found; run `composer require drupal/ai_agents:^1.2` first."

### 2. Locate module and derive identifiers

- Read `<module_path>/<module_name>.info.yml`.
- Plugin ID: snake_case of `AgentName` + `_agent` suffix (e.g., `DatasetExplorer` → `dataset_explorer_agent`). The framework has known bugs around plugin IDs not matching their group; an underscore-cased ID with a clear suffix is safest.
- Class FQN: `Drupal\<module_name>\Plugin\AiAgent\<AgentName>`.

### 3. Update `<module_name>.info.yml` dependencies

Ensure these exist:
- `ai:ai`
- `ai_agents:ai_agents` — `ai_agents` is its own Drupal project (`drupal/ai_agents`), not a submodule of `drupal/ai`. Confirm via `cat <webroot>/modules/contrib/ai_agents/ai_agents.info.yml | grep project`.

### 4. Generate the Agent class

Path: `<module_path>/src/Plugin/AiAgent/<AgentName>.php`.

**Critical patterns:**
- The `#[AiAgent]` attribute has only `id`, `label`, optional `deriver`, optional `module_dependencies`. **No `description` field.**
- `AiAgentBase`'s constructor takes 8 services. Do not redefine `__construct` unless you accept all 8 and pass them to `parent::__construct()`. Use the `parent::create()` pattern below to attach additional services.
- The base class already implements `AiAgentInterface`; only declare `ContainerFactoryPluginInterface` on the subclass.

```php
<?php

declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Drupal\<module_name>\Plugin\AiAgent;

use Drupal\Core\Plugin\ContainerFactoryPluginInterface;
use Drupal\Core\StringTranslation\TranslatableMarkup;
use Drupal\ai_agents\Attribute\AiAgent;
use Drupal\ai_agents\PluginBase\AiAgentBase;
use Drupal\ai_agents\PluginInterfaces\AiAgentInterface;
use Symfony\Component\DependencyInjection\ContainerInterface;

/**
 * TODO: One-paragraph description of what this agent does.
 */
#[AiAgent(
  id: '<plugin_id>',
  label: new TranslatableMarkup('<AgentName> Agent'),
)]
class <AgentName> extends AiAgentBase implements ContainerFactoryPluginInterface {

  // To inject extra services, declare a property with @var (Drupal phpcs
  // requires it; PHP 8.2+ deprecates dynamic properties), then assign in
  // create(). Example:
  //
  //   /**
  //    * The custom service.
  //    *
  //    * @var \Drupal\my_module\MyServiceInterface
  //    */
  //   protected $myService;

  /**
   * {@inheritdoc}
   */
  public static function create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) {
    $parent_instance = parent::create($container, $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition);
    // $parent_instance->myService = $container->get('my_module.my_service');
    return $parent_instance;
  }

  /**
   * {@inheritdoc}
   */
  public function getId() {
    return '<plugin_id>';
  }

  /**
   * {@inheritdoc}
   */
  public function agentsNames() {
    return ['<AgentName> Agent'];
  }

  /**
   * {@inheritdoc}
   */
  public function agentsCapabilities() {
    return [
      '<plugin_id>' => [
        'name' => '<AgentName> Agent',
        'description' => 'TODO: What this agent can do, in 1-2 sentences. The orchestrator reads this to decide when to route a task here.',
        'usage_instructions' => 'TODO: Hints about when to use this agent vs alternatives.',
        'inputs' => [
          'free_text' => [
            'name' => 'Prompt',
            'type' => 'string',
            'description' => 'The user request that this agent should fulfill.',
            'default_value' => '',
            'required' => TRUE,
          ],
        ],
        'outputs' => [
          'answer' => [
            'description' => 'The agent\'s response to the user.',
            'type' => 'string',
          ],
        ],
      ],
    ];
  }

  /**
   * {@inheritdoc}
   */
  public function answerQuestion() {
    // Interactive Q&A handler. If the agent isn't conversational,
    // return a placeholder string.
    return 'TODO: implement <AgentName>::answerQuestion() or return a placeholder.';
  }

  /**
   * {@inheritdoc}
   */
  public function determineSolvability() {
    // Inspect $this->getTask() and/or $this->getChatInput() to decide.
    // Return one of: AiAgentInterface::JOB_SOLVABLE, JOB_NOT_SOLVABLE,
    // JOB_NEEDS_ANSWERS, JOB_SHOULD_ANSWER_QUESTION, JOB_INFORMS.
    return AiAgentInterface::JOB_SOLVABLE;
  }

  /**
   * {@inheritdoc}
   */
  public function solve() {
    // Typical pattern: load a YAML prompt and dispatch via the agent helper.
    // The prompt file is referenced by name without the .yml extension; the
    // helper resolves the module and prompts/<plugin_id>/ directory for you.
    // $context = ['User request' => $this->getTask()->getDescription()];
    // $result = $this->agentHelper->runSubAgent('determineTask', $context);
    // Branch on $result, mutate state as needed, return a string summary.
    return 'TODO: implement <AgentName>::solve()';
  }

}
```

### 5. Generate prompt directory and a placeholder YAML prompt

**Critical: prompt files are YAML, not txt.** The directory name must match the plugin ID exactly.

- Create directory: `<module_path>/prompts/<plugin_id>/`
- Create one starter prompt: `<module_path>/prompts/<plugin_id>/determineTask.yml`:

  ```yaml
  # Initial task analysis prompt loaded by runSubAgent().
  # See <webroot>/modules/contrib/ai_agents/prompts/field_type_agent/ for real examples.
  #
  # AiAgentBase::actionYamlPrompts() reads the NESTED keys below. It builds the
  # system prompt from prompt.introduction, then appends each prompt.formats entry
  # (JSON-encoded) as the required RFC8259 response shape, the optional
  # prompt.possible_actions list, and optional one_shot_learning_examples. There is
  # no flat `prompt:` string and no `output_schema:` key — those are not read.
  preferred_model: gpt-4o
  preferred_llm: openai
  is_triage: true
  weight: 0
  name: Determine <AgentName> Task
  description: |
    TODO: One-paragraph description of what this sub-agent decides and which
    follow-up sub-agents each action routes to.
  prompt:
    introduction: >
      You are <AgentName> Agent. Your job is TODO: describe role. Based on the
      task description and comments, decide which action to take.
    possible_actions:
      do_thing: The user wants the agent to perform its primary action.
      information: You need more information from the end user.
    formats:
      - action: action id from the list above, so do_thing or information.
        summary: A short human-readable summary of what you decided.
    one_shot_learning_examples:
      - action: do_thing
        summary: TODO example summary for a do_thing decision.
      - action: information
        summary: TODO example asking the user for the missing detail.
  ```

The exact YAML schema varies by use case — read `<webroot>/modules/contrib/ai_agents/prompts/field_type_agent/answerQuestion.yml` and `determineFieldTask.yml` to see real patterns before filling in.

### 6. Generate a unit test stub

Path: `<module_path>/tests/src/Unit/Plugin/AiAgent/<AgentName>Test.php`. Tests:
- The plugin attribute exposes the expected `id` and `label`.
- `agentsCapabilities()` returns an array keyed by the plugin ID with the required keys (`name`, `description`, `inputs`, `outputs`).

A behavioral test of `solve()` requires mocking 8+ services and the LLM provider — defer to a kernel test once the agent's logic is real.

### 7. Cache rebuild

Run `ddev drush cr`.

### 8. Verify runtime discovery

Don't report done on scaffold output alone — `AiAgentBase`'s abstract methods (`agentsNames()`, `answerQuestion()`) and the 8-service constructor only fail at instantiation time. Run:

```bash
ddev drush ev "\$a = \Drupal::service('plugin.manager.ai_agents')->createInstance('<plugin_id>'); echo get_class(\$a) . PHP_EOL; print_r(array_keys(\$a->agentsCapabilities()['<plugin_id>']));"
```

Expected: prints the FQN and the capability keys (`name`, `description`, `usage_instructions`, `inputs`, `outputs`). Any "abstract method" fatal or DI failure surfaces here.

### 9. Print next steps

1. Read `<webroot>/modules/contrib/ai_agents/src/Plugin/AiAgent/FieldType.php` end-to-end — it's the canonical example for code-defined agents and demonstrates the helpers you'll use (`runAiProvider`, `runSubAgent`, `actionYamlPrompts`).
2. Refine `agentsCapabilities()` — the `description`, `usage_instructions`, `inputs`, and `outputs` arrays are how the orchestrator routes tasks to your agent. Be specific.
3. Replace `prompts/<plugin_id>/determineTask.yml` with real per-operation prompts (one YAML file per LLM call your `solve()` makes). Pattern after `field_type_agent`'s prompt files.
4. Implement `solve()` — typically a determine-then-act flow using `runSubAgent()` to drive YAML prompts and decode structured output.
5. Verify discovery: `ddev drush ev "var_dump(\Drupal::service('plugin.manager.ai_agents')->getDefinitions()['<plugin_id>'] ?? 'NOT FOUND');"`
6. Test instantiation: `ddev drush ev "\$a = \Drupal::service('plugin.manager.ai_agents')->createInstance('<plugin_id>'); var_dump(\$a->agentsCapabilities());"`

## Pitfall checks before reporting done

- [ ] Attribute uses ONLY `id`, `label` (and optional `deriver`/`module_dependencies`) — no `description` field.
- [ ] `prompts/<plugin_id>/` directory matches plugin ID exactly (lowercase, snake_case).
- [ ] Prompt files are `.yml` (not `.txt`) — one per LLM operation.
- [ ] `<module_name>.info.yml` includes the right `ai_agents` dependency declaration.
- [ ] Plugin path is `src/Plugin/AiAgent/` (PascalCase, singular).
- [ ] Agent class declares only `ContainerFactoryPluginInterface` (not `AiAgentInterface` — base provides it).
- [ ] No constructor override unless adding new services (and even then, accept all 8 base services).
- [ ] `determineSolvability()` returns one of the actual JOB_* constants on `AiAgentInterface` (not the fictional `JOB_NEEDS_MORE_INFO`).
- [ ] Both `answerQuestion()` and `agentsNames()` are implemented — they're abstract in `AiAgentBase` and instantiation fails without them.
- [ ] `agentsCapabilities()` returns an array keyed by plugin ID with `name`/`description`/`usage_instructions`/`inputs`/`outputs`.
- [ ] `drush cr` ran without error.
